description:
You are a data-scientist about to launch a heavy data-cleaning job and you want to archive a quick “before” snapshot of the workstation’s state for future troubleshooting.  
Create a single plain-text log that captures the raw output of SIX specific diagnostic commands and a companion summary file.

1. Make sure the directory  
   /home/user/monitoring_logs  
   exists (create it if it doesn’t).

2. Inside that directory create a file named  
   /home/user/monitoring_logs/pre_clean_diag.log  

   • The file must contain the output of the following six commands in the exact order shown below.  
   • Prepend each command’s output with the EXACT header line shown (including the surrounding triple dashes and the uppercase spelling).  
   • Leave one completely blank line AFTER every header line and also one completely blank line AFTER every command’s output block.  
   • No other text or blank lines should appear.  
   • The automated checker will look only at the headers and their ordering, so the actual command output can vary with the live system, but the headers and blank-line layout are mandatory.

   Required header sequence (one per command):

   --- DATE ---  
   --- HOSTNAME ---  
   --- UNAME ---  
   --- UPTIME ---  
   --- MEMORY ---  
   --- DISK ---

   The commands that must immediately follow each header are:

   1) date  
   2) hostname  
   3) uname -a  
   4) uptime  
   5) free -m  
   6) df -h  

   Example (layout only):

   --- DATE ---

   Wed Mar 13 17:02:25 UTC 2024

   --- HOSTNAME ---

   mymachine

   …and so on for all six sections.

3. Create a second text file called  
   /home/user/monitoring_logs/pre_clean_diag_summary.txt  

   • This summary must contain exactly six lines.  
   • Each line must be the FIRST line of the corresponding command’s output, written in the same order as in the main log (no headers, no extra blank lines).  
   • For instance, for “date” it will usually be a single line timestamp; for “free -m” it will be the header line beginning with “total”.

When finished you should have the directory plus two files whose formats match the rules above. The test harness will fail if:

• Any header is misspelled, missing, out of order, or not separated by exactly one blank line.  
• Either file is missing or located elsewhere.  
• The summary file does not have exactly six non-blank lines.

Do NOT display the contents of the files on the console—simply create them.
truth:
Expected artefacts that the test suite will check:

1. Directory:
   /home/user/monitoring_logs              (must exist, permissions 700 or more permissive)

2. File:
   /home/user/monitoring_logs/pre_clean_diag.log

   Mandatory header skeleton inside that file, in this precise order, each followed by a blank line:

   Line 1:  --- DATE ---
   Line 3:  --- HOSTNAME ---
   Line 5:  --- UNAME ---
   Line 7:  --- UPTIME ---
   Line 9:  --- MEMORY ---
   Line 11: --- DISK ---

   (Because every header is followed by a blank line, these header lines will occupy the odd-numbered lines shown above, assuming each command prints at least one line.)

3. File:
   /home/user/monitoring_logs/pre_clean_diag_summary.txt

   • Must contain exactly 6 non-empty lines (line count only verified).  
   • No blank or whitespace-only lines allowed.

No other files are required.
